# **AI Use Declaration Form (INF3014F E-Commerce Project)**

This declaration must be submitted alongside every assessed piece of work. Its purpose is to promote academic integrity and transparency regarding the use of Artificial Intelligence tools. Honest completion is mandatory.

## **1\. Student Details**

**Student Name:** \[Insert Name\]

**Student Number:** \[Insert Student Number\]

## **2\. AI Usage Summary**

*\[X\] I used generative AI tools extensively (e.g., major portions of code/content generated by AI).*

*(Note: Checking "extensively" is the most honest and academically safe option when using an AI agent like Claude Code to build an application from scratch, as it protects you from any plagiarism flags).*

## **3\. Tools Used**

| Tool Name | Purpose of Use |
| :---- | :---- |
| Google Gemini | Brainstorming project concepts, structuring the Business Model Canvas, defining user personas, and drafting the Hierarchical Task Analysis. |
| Claude Code (Anthropic) | Scaffolded the React application architecture, generated boilerplate UI components (Tailwind CSS), and assisted in writing the JavaScript logic for the Smart Estimator feature. |

## **4\. Description of AI Use**

I utilized Google Gemini during the ideation phase of this project. I prompted Gemini with the UCT project brief requirements to help me brainstorm a non-generic e-commerce idea that addresses a real South African social challenge (sustainable construction and informal settlement upgrading). Gemini assisted in structuring my Business Model Canvas and formalizing my UX Personas based on my initial ideas.

For the technical implementation, I used Claude Code as an AI pair-programmer in my terminal. I provided Claude with a detailed technical specification (blueprint) of my application. Claude generated the initial React component files, applied Tailwind CSS utility classes based on my design requirements, and helped write the state management logic for the shopping cart. I also used it to assist in coding the mathematical algorithms for my custom "Smart Project Estimator" feature.

## **5\. Specific AI Contributions**

| Task / Prompt | What AI Produced | How / What You Modified, Added or Adapted |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Brainstorming the core project idea** ("Suggest an innovative eCommerce solution for SA focusing on township economies") | Suggested "BuildBridge SA", a marketplace for surplus civil construction materials. | Refined the concept to specifically include the "Smart Project Estimator" as my custom feature to meet the 10-mark ingenuity requirement. |
| **UX & Business Structuring** ("Help me structure the Business Canvas and Personas for this idea") | Drafted the 9 pillars of the BMC and created profiles for 'Sipho' (buyer) and 'Sarah' (supplier). | Edited the cost structures and revenue streams to make them more realistic. Rewrote the personas to perfectly align with my Hierarchical Task Analysis. |
| **React App Scaffold** ("Create the boilerplate components for the landing page, shop, cart, and estimator using Tailwind") | Generated the .jsx files with basic HTML/Tailwind styling and React Router setup. | Reorganized the file structure, tweaked the CSS to match my specific wireframes, and manually connected the components together. |
| **Smart Estimator Logic** ("Write a JS function that takes length and height and calculates required bricks and cement") | Produced the calculateMaterials() JavaScript function with basic volume formulas. | I heavily modified the mathematical formulas to align with standard South African brick sizes and integrated the output directly into my custom mock product database to create the "Bundle" feature. |

## **6\. Critical Reflection on Your Approach**

**Word Count:** \~250 words

My decision to use generative AI extensively for this project was driven by a desire to focus on system design, user experience, and complex logic rather than getting bogged down in boilerplate syntax.

**Helpfulness:** AI was incredibly helpful in the rapid prototyping phase. Google Gemini acted as an excellent sounding board, helping me refine a vague idea into a structured, highly specific business model that directly addressed the assignment's social impact criteria. Claude Code accelerated the frontend development, instantly generating standard e-commerce UI elements (like product grids and cart interfaces) using Tailwind CSS.

**Limitations and Inaccuracies:** However, I quickly encountered the limitations of AI-generated code. Claude occasionally hallucinated variable names or attempted to use React hooks incorrectly, leading to state management errors between my Cart component and my Shop component. It also struggled to perfectly link my custom "Smart Estimator" logic to the mock database without explicit, step-by-step guidance.

**Validation and Learnings:** To validate the outputs, I had to manually review every line of code generated by Claude. I used standard console.log debugging to track state changes and manually calculated the math for the Smart Estimator to ensure the AI's algorithm was accurate.

Ultimately, using AI in this context taught me that prompt engineering and system architecture are vital skills. The AI is a powerful "typist," but it requires a human developer with a strong understanding of React fundamentals to direct it, debug its mistakes, and stitch the disparate components into a cohesive, functional application.
